title: Administrative Burden Baseline update 2025 - Canada.ca
content:
## Administrative Burden Baseline: Update 2025
Regulatory organizations are required through the Administrative Burden Baseline to provide a count of the requirements in federal regulations that impose an administrative burden on business.
All federal regulations that are administered by the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) that have requirements that impose administrative burden on business are identified below. The total 2025 ABB count is 189. The total June 30th, 2014, the baseline count is 42.
Between July 1, 2015 and June 30, 2016, PHAC's total count of administrative burden requirements changed as a result of the coming into force of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations (HPTR) and the repeal of the Human Pathogen Importation Regulations .
- Administrative Burden Baseline Count for 2025
- Regulatory information

## Administrative Burden Baseline Count for 2025
Title of the Regulation
Potable Water on Board Trains, Vessels, Aircraft and Buses Regulations
Title of the Regulation
Human Pathogen and Toxins Regulations
Previous years' counts for PHAC are available upon request.
Regulatory Affairs Public Health Agency of Canada 100 Colonnade Road Ottawa, ON K1A 0K9
Email: pathogens.pathogenes@phac-aspc.gc.ca

## Regulatory information
Consult the Public Health Agency of Canada's acts and regulations web page for:
- a list of acts and regulations administered by PHAC
- further information on PHAC's implementation of government-wide regulatory management initiatives
Consult the following for links to the Cabinet Directive on Regulation and supporting policies and guidance, and for information on government-wide regulatory initiatives implemented by regulatory organizations across the Government of Canada:
- Federal regulatory management
- Learn more about regulatory cooperation
To learn about upcoming or ongoing consultations on proposed federal regulations, visit:
- Consulting with Canadians
